questions.question: How do you create a new inline board view on an existing page?

options.text: Click the + button and select Board from the sidebar
options.explanation: This creates a new full-page board. Inline boards are created using a slash command.

options.text: Type '/Board view' and press Enter
options.explanation: The slash command '/Board view' allows you to insert a board view directly into the current page.

options.text: Drag a full-page board into the page
options.explanation: Dragging a full-page board creates a sub-page, not an inline block.

options.text: Go to Settings > Layout > Insert Board
options.explanation: Board views are inserted using the slash command menu, not the main database settings.

questions.question: By default, how does a board view group database items if a 'Status' property exists?

options.text: It groups by the 'Created time' property
options.explanation: While you can sort by time, the default grouping logic prioritizes specific properties like Status.

options.text: It groups by the 'Status' property
options.explanation: The article states that by default, a board is grouped by a status property if one exists.

options.text: It groups randomly
options.explanation: Board views are structured to organize data logically, not randomly.

options.text: It groups by the 'Assignee' property
options.explanation: Assignee is a valid grouping property, but Status is the default when available.

questions.question: You want to group your board by 'Priority', but this property doesn't exist in your database. Where do you go to create it?

options.text: Settings > Group
options.explanation: The Group settings menu allows you to *change* the grouping property, but it cannot create a new property type.

options.text: Settings > Edit properties
options.explanation: To add a new property type like 'Select' for Priority, you must navigate to the database properties settings.

options.text: Settings > Layout
options.explanation: The Layout settings are for card size and page opening behavior, not property definitions.

options.text: Right-click the column header and select 'New property'
options.explanation: You cannot create new property types directly from the column header; you must go to the database settings.

questions.question: If your database contains a 'Number' property, which calculation option allows you to find the middle value of the column?

options.text: Average
options.explanation: Average calculates the mean by summing all values and dividing by the count, not the middle value.

options.text: Range
options.explanation: Range calculates the difference between the highest and lowest numbers, not the median.

options.text: Median
options.explanation: Median calculates the middle number in an ordered list of values, which is distinct from the Average.

options.text: Sum
options.explanation: Sum adds all values together, providing a total rather than a central tendency.

questions.question: When displaying images on board cards, what does the 'Fit image' toggle do?

options.text: Crops the image to fill the entire card frame
options.explanation: This describes the behavior when 'Fit image' is turned OFF.

options.text: Shows the whole image within the frame of the card
options.explanation: When turned ON, the entire image fits within the card boundaries without cropping.

options.text: Removes the image entirely
options.explanation: The toggle controls how the image is displayed, not whether it is shown.

options.text: Only displays the page cover image
options.explanation: The 'Fit image' toggle works regardless of whether you are showing the cover, content, or files.

questions.question: How do you hide a specific group (column) in a board view?

options.text: Click the column heading and select 'Hide' from the menu
options.explanation: You must click the ••• icon specifically to access the 'Hide group' option.

options.text: Click ••• next to the heading and select 'Hide group'
options.explanation: The specific menu option for hiding a group is found next to the column header.

options.text: Drag the column to the bottom of the screen
options.explanation: Dragging moves the column position, it does not hide it.

options.text: Go to Settings > Group and uncheck the column
options.explanation: While hidden columns appear in the Group settings, you must hide them via the column header menu first.

questions.question: Which action allows you to rearrange the columns in a board view?

options.text: Click and drag the column heading left or right
options.explanation: You can click and hold on a column heading to drag it to reorder the columns.

options.text: Double-click the column header to swap positions
options.explanation: Double-clicking does not trigger a reorder; drag-and-drop is the supported method.

options.text: Select 'Reorder' from the settings menu
options.explanation: The settings menu does not contain a generic reorder tool; you must interact directly with the columns.

options.text: Cut and paste the column title into a new location
options.explanation: There is no text-based copy/paste method for moving columns.

questions.question: To convert an existing inline database into a top-level full-page database, what must you do?

options.text: Select 'Turn into inline' from the settings menu
options.explanation: That command converts a full-page board into an inline one, not the reverse.

options.text: Drag the inline database block into the sidebar
options.explanation: Dragging an inline database block into the sidebar creates it as a new top-level page.

options.text: Double-click the block and select 'New Page'
options.explanation: Double-clicking opens the database for viewing/editing, not for converting to a page.

options.text: Rename the database file to end in '.page'
options.explanation: Notion handles the file structure automatically; there is no manual file extension method.

questions.question: What is the term used for the second layer of grouping within a specific group in a board view?

options.text: Sub-columns
options.explanation: Board views use groups and sub-groups, not sub-columns.

options.text: Child cards
options.explanation: Child cards refers to the items within the group, not the grouping layer itself.

options.text: Sub-groups
options.explanation: The article explicitly refers to this second layer of organization as 'sub-groups'.

options.text: Nested properties
options.explanation: Nested properties is not a term used for the visual structure of the board.

questions.question: How can you change the order of properties displayed on the front of your board cards?

options.text: Drag them in the settings menu
options.explanation: The settings menu allows you to show/hide, but not reorder properties.

options.text: Use the ⋮⋮ icon to drag them
options.explanation: The vertical ellipsis (⋮⋮) icon in the Property visibility menu allows you to reorder properties.

options.text: Click 'Reorder' in Layout settings
options.explanation: Layout settings control card size and image display, not property order.

options.text: Rename them in the property list
options.explanation: Renaming properties changes their label, not their position on the card.

questions.question: If you try to group by a property that doesn't exist, what happens?

options.text: An error message appears and the board remains empty
options.explanation: Notion is designed to be helpful; it creates the property for you automatically.

options.text: A new property with default settings is created automatically
options.explanation: If no Status, Select, Person, etc., property exists, Notion creates a new Status property for you.

options.text: Nothing happens and the board stays empty
options.explanation: The board will attempt to populate based on the new property once created.

options.text: It defaults to grouping by 'Name'
options.explanation: The default fallback is specifically a Status property, not Name.

questions.question: Is it currently possible to group a board view by a Formula property?

options.text: Yes, by using the 'Formula' type in the Group settings
options.explanation: The Help Center explicitly states this is 'Not currently' supported.

options.text: No, this feature is not currently supported
options.explanation: The article includes a direct response stating that grouping by relation or formula is not currently supported.

options.text: Yes, but only if the formula returns a date value
options.explanation: The limitation applies to all formula properties regardless of their return type.

options.text: No, but it will be available in the next update
options.explanation: While the team mentions it is a 'legit use case', they do not promise an immediate future timeline.

questions.question: Which calculation shows the time gap between the oldest and newest edit or creation time?

options.text: Earliest date
options.explanation: Earliest date shows the date of the oldest item, not the gap between dates.

options.text: Latest date
options.explanation: Latest date shows the date of the newest item, not the gap between dates.

options.text: Date range
options.explanation: The 'Date range' option calculates the difference (gap) between the earliest and latest dates.

options.text: Count unique values
options.explanation: This calculates the number of unique items, unrelated to time differences.

questions.question: What are the three available card sizes in a board view?

options.text: Small, Medium, Large
options.explanation: The Layout settings offer exactly these three options for card size.

options.text: Tiny, Normal, Huge
options.explanation: These are not the standard terminology used in the Notion interface.

options.text: Compact, Standard, Expanded
options.explanation: Notion uses specific size labels, not these descriptive terms.

options.text: Mini, Average, Maxi
options.explanation: Notion uses Small, Medium, and Large.

questions.question: How can you temporarily hide a 'Done' column to keep your board clean?

options.text: Archive the column
options.explanation: There is no 'Archive' action for columns; you must use the Hide function.

options.text: Delete the column
options.explanation: Hiding keeps the data available; deleting removes it permanently.

options.text: Hide the group
options.explanation: Clicking the ••• menu and selecting 'Hide group' temporarily removes the column.

options.text: Drag the column to the sidebar
options.explanation: Dragging a group to the sidebar is not a standard action; it should be dragged to a different location or hidden.
